step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("correlation between humeral and glenoid bone lesions in chronic anterior post-traumatic shoulder dislocations")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

First write 2 sentences agreeing or disagreeing with prior conclusions, incorporating newly found evidence.
Then give final choice using \boxed{{letter}}
Based on the retrieved evidence, we confirm that both humeral and glenoidal lesions are frequently encountered together in chronic anterior post-traumatic shoulder dislocations. High-energy impacts causing anterior dislocations often induce simultaneous bony defects—the humeral head develops a Hill-Sachs lesion due to impaction against the glenoid, while the glenoid itself experiences bone loss from repetitive stress, attrition, or fracture. These correlated pathologies form part of the "glenoid track" model, where the size and shape of the Hill-Sachs lesion determine whether it engages with residual glenoid bone during movement, thus predicting instability risks and requiring dual-targeted surgical correction. Therefore, I agree with the original assertion regarding a strong correlation between humeral and glenoidal bony defects.
